steps.title: Understand how sample size affects margin of error
steps.content: The margin of error is inversely proportional to the square root of the sample size. In the formula $\text{MOE} = z^* \sqrt{p(1-p)/n}$, we see that n is in the denominator under a square root, so larger sample sizes produce smaller margins of error.

steps.title: Calculate the effect of doubling the sample size
steps.content:
The sample size doubles from 159 to 318. The margin of error scales by:
$$\sqrt{\frac{159}{318}} = \sqrt{\frac{1}{2}} \approx 0.707$$
Therefore, the new margin of error is approximately 5.05% × 0.707 ≈ 3.57%, which is lower than the original 5.05%.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
